description: Pinned perf baseline for the PFSF CPU microbench. scripts/pfsf_perf_gate.py compares build/pfsf-bench/results.json against these budgets. Every primitive has a Java-ref upper bound (java_ns_per_op_max) and a lower bound on the native speedup when the .so is loaded (native_over_java_min). Leaving native_over_java_min as null disables the native check for that row (used when the .so is unavailable on the current runner). Regression policy: any java_ns_per_op exceeding its max by more than tolerance_pct fails CI; any native_over_java below its min by more than tolerance_pct also fails.

runner.note: Budgets calibrated on GitHub Actions ubuntu-22.04 runners (2-core @ 3.25 GHz, 7 GB RAM). Re-pin on runner-hardware changes via scripts/pfsf_perf_gate.py --update-baseline.

primitives.name: normalize_soa6_64k
primitives.note: Bandwidth-bound 64k × 7 floats (source+rcomp+rtens+4·cond). AVX2 target_clones path in libpfsf_compute expected to hit 2×+; budget is conservative 1.8× to absorb noise.

primitives.name: chebyshev_table_64
primitives.note: Tiny 64-entry table — JNI boundary cost can dominate. Don't demand a native speedup here; only check Java-ref stability.

primitives.name: apply_wind_bias_64k
primitives.note: Six direction passes, each a scalar multiply; tight SIMD target.

primitives.name: tick50k_surrogate
primitives.note: The v0.3d delivery gate. 100 reps × (normalize + wind bias + omega table) on 50k voxels. native_over_java_min=1.4 matches the M6 acceptance criterion.
